title: festuca beckeri subsp. polesica
intro: Festuca beckeri subsp. polesica, also known as a type of grass, is a lesser-known species of fescue, a genus of flowering plants in the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Festuca beckeri subsp. polesica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive issues, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. However, specific herbal preparations and dosages for this plant are not widely documented, but it is often used in traditional medicine to prepare teas, infusions, and poultices.
